nodes.title: Lora
widgets_values:
Don`t delete/turn off the first Lora:

1. ltx-2.3-22b-distilled-lora-dynamic_fro09_avg_rank_105_bf16.safetensors
   Destilled Lora for low steps generation.

Feel free to use any additional Loras you like.

nodes.title: Preview First Frame Resized [[P:02 Image to Video]]
I2V/FLF Resize Longer Edge [[P:02 Image to Video]]
How to use this workflow
widgets_values:
Preperation:

1. Download all models and save them at the specified directories.
2. Select/check in every blue model loader node your downloaded models.
   (Please check this twice, a lot of reported issues are caused by model mismatch). 


Run:
1. Go to group "Input" and select your options in the black switch node (use the Select Option table to find the 
   right options).
2. Set your values in the brown input nodes like "Clip Length" and "Seed" and make all inputs in the highlighted 
   (not muted) yellow subgroup "P" (brown input nodes and green loader nodes).
3. Enter your prompts.
4. Choose/select optional Loras.
5. Click start.

nodes.title: Troubleshooting
widgets_values:
Troubleshooting:
Turn off Nodes 2.0 in comfyui.
Make sure your GPU driver, comfyui system, comfyui (>= v0.16.3) and all your custom nodes are up to date.

Custom node conflicts:
Easy workaround for a clean custom nodes folder: Copy/save your custom_nodes folder under a different name. Delete all custom nodes in your original folder (except manager). Restart comfyui and install all missing nodes.

If node conflicts still arise or nodes not installable, check the github pages for workarounds. 
If you struggle with "unsolvable" issues you might consider to install a separate comfyui system just for video generation, like "Comfyui-Easy-Install.

If you get OOM VRAM errors reduce resolution/clip length.
If you get server crashes caused by OOM RAM issues, check/increase your swap file.

nodes.title: Cut OFF Start Frames [[P:Last Frame input]]
Cut OFF End Frames [[P:Last Frame input]]
Input Help Subgroup "P"
widgets_values:
T2V: Select the longer edge via slider node and Portrait = true (or fale for landscape mode).

I2V: Load your First Frame image and set Resize Longer Edge. Your image gets automatically resized and cropped to 16:9/9:16.
     Make sure your start image is simillar to 16:9/9:16 format to avoid excessive cropping.

V2V: This option extends your input video by taking the "style" of the given video.
     Load your video and set Resize Longer Edge. Please follow the same instructions for resizing and cropping as for I2V.
     Clip length = your start video length + desired extension length.

FLF: Load your Last Frame image in addition to your First Frame image and follow the same instructions for resizing and cropping as for I2V.
     Cut OFF Start/End Frames: FLF option often generates some very distorted frames at start or end. With these settings you can cut off 
     automatically unwanted frames. "Cut OFF End Frames = 24" will cut off the last second of your video for example (24 frames per second).

Audio input: Load your audio file and set clip length according to your Audio length.

nodes.title: Attention: Group Name "P" and Node Titel Tags
widgets_values:
Attention:
Group name and node titel tags are essential for automatic switching between options! 

Don`t edit group name "P".
Don`t edit node titel tags like: [[P:02 Image to Video]] for example.

nodes.title: Select Options
widgets_values:
Select the options in the black switch node according to the following workflow table:


Workflow:                           |Select options:
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Text to Video                       |01 Text to Video
Text + Audio to Video               |01 Text to Video + Audio input
                                    |
Image to Video                      |02 Image to Video
Image + Audio to Video              |02 Image to Video + Audio input
                                    |
First/Last Frame to Video           |02 Image to Video + Last Frame input
First/Last Frame + Audio to Video   |02 Image to Video + Last Frame input + Audio input
                                    |
Video to Video                      |03 Video to Video (this option extends your start video)


Attention:
Make sure you select the right switches according to the above table only (otherwise you might get error messages). According to your selection all necessary inputs are highlighted automatically in subgroup "P".

nodes.title: All-In-One LTX-2.3 Workflow, runs perfect with RTX 3060 GPU with 12 GB VRAM
widgets_values:
Simple to use all-In-One LTX-2 workflow with options for Text/Image/FLF/Video/Audio to Video.

Requirements:
- GPU with 12 GB VRAM (some users reported it runs with 8 GB too)
- 32 GB VRAM
- Swap file size: 64 - 128 GB


Runs very fast: 5 second (1280 x 864) Video: < 10 minutes.
Generation of long high quality videos in one run possible: 10 - 20 seconds without any issues,

Brilliant audio generation (speech/sound) with LTX-2.

Simple to use workflow:
- easy switching between all options with Orchestrator Node Muter,
- all steps highly automated: no manual frame or aspect ratio calculations necessary,
- easy to set inputs by predefined sliders (no risk to set wrong inputs, like wrong aspect ratios or wrong frame 
  counts).

This workflow ensures strict compliance with the LTX-2.3 specifications:
- aspect ratios of 16:9/9:16 only,
- Width and Hight divisible by 32.


Important:
This workflow is intended for advanced comfyui users who know how to install and operate the system and are able to resolve basic system errors themselves, like as node conflicts, or general system issues.

nodes.title: About this workflow
widgets_values:
This workflow is mainly based on the fantastic LTX-2.3 workflows of Urabewe:
https://akumaai.cc/models/2443867/ltx-23-22b-gguf-workflows-12gb-vram.
As far as I know, those were the first workflows running LTX-2 with 12 GB VRAM. All credits goes to the original creator.

My job was only to combine and organise the different workflows in a simple to use all-in-one design.
With workflow version 3.0 I created a pretty complex swich logic "under the hood" to manage all options and keep the "user interface" as simple as possible.

The new version now strictly adheres to the LTX 2.3 specifications for aspect ratios and should deliver significantly better results (less artefacts, distortions, etc.).

inputs.text: A medium close-up shot features a Caucasian man with a closely shaven head and face, wearing a black baseball cap with "PNTR" in white letters on the front, and a dark grey t-shirt with "JUST DO IT" visible across his chest. A small black microphone is clipped to his shirt collar. He is positioned slightly to the left of the frame, looking intently downwards and to his right, his eyes focused off-camera. His facial expression is one of deep concentration, with his brow slightly furrowed. As he looks down, a quick sniff sound is heard, and then he speaks with a deep male voice and a slightly frustrated tone, saying, "I think it's so bad." The camera remains static throughout, maintaining a shallow depth of field, which keeps the man in sharp focus while the background is softly blurred, revealing a light-colored wall with white wooden shelving or trim, and a partially open white wooden door on the right. After a brief pause, another short, audible sniff is heard. The man then continues to speak, his voice maintaining the same quality, as he states, "So bad. So bad." He elaborates further, emphasizing his point with a final statement, "This got to be, it's got to be the worst tool I've ever seen."
